note: Friday 5/8 daily scrape. NEW lead discovery (5/5–5/8 announcements) plus signal refresh on 3 hot prior-week leads. 4 new leads scoring 65+ across 2 pipelines (pr-marketing, legal-freelance). Quality over quantity — multiple post-funding press cycles already covered in 5/4–5/7 reports (Corgi, RadixArk, Sierra, Astranis, JuliaHub, etc.) so the 5/8 surface area was deliberately narrow. PR-marketing tilt: Fazeshift Series A (5/7) is the strongest single new lead this week — F-Prime-led AI accounts-receivable, customers include Sigma Computing/Snyk/Clipboard Health, no in-house comms named in 5/7 press cycle. Legal-freelance: Hayden Industrial fractional GC posting is a clean Mark-direct-pitch opportunity (200 employees, $300M revenue, 1–2 days/week, monthly retainer). NO new fund-formation, legal-consulting, coaching-ops, media, ai-consulting, or pr-freelance leads scoring 60+ today (job-board churn was thin; agency-event pipeline was dominated by raises already captured 5/6–5/7).

leads.name: Fazeshift
leads.industry: AI Accounts Receivable / Autonomous Finance Agents — Fintech B2B (AI-native platform deploying autonomous agents to execute end-to-end AR workflows: invoicing, collections, payment matching, reconciliation across existing systems)
leads.location: United States (HQ unspecified in 5/7 release; F-Prime portco)
leads.fundingStage: $17M Series A announced May 7, 2026 (total funding $22M). Led by F-Prime; participation from Gradient Ventures (Google's early-stage AI fund), Y Combinator, Wayfinder Ventures, Pioneer Fund, Ritual Capital, plus prominent angels.

contacts.name: Fazeshift Founders (CEO/CTO publicly named in 5/7 BusinessWire / Crunchbase News / PYMNTS / Unite.AI coverage — verify exact names from release)
contacts.title: Co-founders

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-08 (BusinessWire / Crunchbase News / PYMNTS / Unite.AI / F-Prime Capital blog)
leads.notes: STRONGEST NEW LEAD this week (combined 91, A-tier). The signal stack is exceptional: (a) F-Prime is a top-tier fintech-specialist VC that absolutely expects portcos to invest in tier-1 PR for the Series A moment, (b) the customer roster — Sigma Computing, Snyk, Meter, Clipboard Health, plus '8 unicorns' — is unambiguous proof-of-traction press copy, (c) the 12x-revenue-growth narrative is a clean fintech-trade-press hook, (d) 'autonomous AI agents executing finance workflows' is a 2026 narrative arc that lands cleanly with WSJ/Bloomberg/Forbes enterprise-AI beats AND with fintech-trade press (PYMNTS already covered, Forbes Fintech / Finextra / Tearsheet are next-cycle targets). Buyer fit: 45/50 — fintech AI B2B (perfect FS/Tech industry match), Series A stage with $22M total raise puts them squarely in $5–25k/mo agency-retainer tier (might stretch to $15–25k given F-Prime backing). Comms-relationship odds: STRONG. The 5/7 BusinessWire/Crunchbase/PYMNTS/Unite.AI coverage names ONLY the company and product — no Head of Comms, no VP Marketing, no Director of Communications quoted anywhere. Same 'YC alum, Series A, no in-house PR yet' signal we saw on Reserv (5/4, 87) and Corgi (5/7, 89). Press cycle on day 1 (announcement was 5/7, today is 5/8) — peak outreach window 5/7–5/17. Pitch threading: (i) AI-native autonomous-finance category-creation narrative as fintech-trade press hook (PYMNTS, Tearsheet, Finextra, FintechFutures, AFP Online, plus general fintech beats at TechCrunch/Forbes/Bloomberg), (ii) customer-logo-driven case-study series (Sigma, Snyk, Clipboard Health each their own placement opportunity over 6 months), (iii) thought-leadership from CEO on 'autonomous finance is the new SaaS' for CFO/finance-leader trade press (CFO Magazine, CFO Dive, Strategic Finance), (iv) F-Prime portfolio-company narrative cross-promotion (Gradient + a16z + F-Prime each have their own portco-press cycles). Outreach lead-touch: founders (verify names from BusinessWire release). Time-sensitive: outreach in next 48 hours optimal. SF-or-NY HQ likely (unverified). DEFINITELY pursue — this is the Reserv/Corgi-tier fit-pattern.
leads.lastActivity: $17M Series A announced May 7, 2026 (BusinessWire / Crunchbase News / PYMNTS / Unite.AI / F-Prime / Yahoo Finance / Bakersfield)

leads.name: Ethos (London-based AI expert network)
leads.industry: AI-Powered Expert Networking Platform — B2B (voice-onboarding AI talent agent serving hedge funds, asset managers, consultancies, IB; competing with GLG, AlphaSights, Third Bridge in the $2B+ expert-network category)
leads.location: London, UK (HQ); US expansion planned per 5/6 funding announcement
leads.fundingStage: $22.75M Series A announced May 6, 2026. Led by Andreessen Horowitz; participation from General Catalyst, XTX Markets, Evantic Capital, Common Magic.

contacts.name: James Lo
contacts.title: Co-founder (formerly McKinsey then SoftBank — worked on WeWork and Arm transformations; named in 5/6 TechCrunch coverage)

contacts.name: Daniel Mankowitz
contacts.title: Co-founder (formerly DeepMind AI researcher — YouTube video compression, Gemini, AlphaDev sorting; named in 5/6 TechCrunch coverage)

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-08 (TechCrunch / TheNextWeb / FinSMEs / TechFundingNews / Tech.eu)
leads.notes: A-tier (combined 84). What makes Ethos genuinely interesting for pr-marketing — and not just 'another a16z-backed AI Series A' — is that their BUYERS ARE MARK'S BUYER PROFILE: hedge funds, asset managers, alternative-investments firms, IB advisory. Expert networks are the connective tissue of FS research workflows. So the press strategy isn't 'AI hype' — it's 'how does a hedge fund evaluate an expert-network alternative,' which IS the trade-press conversation Mark's playbook is built to drive. Buyer fit: 41/50 — perfect industry match (AI + B2B serving FS), Series A stage at $22.75M sits squarely in $10–25k/mo retainer tier, founders' credentials (McKinsey/SoftBank + DeepMind) make for credible thought-leadership material. Comms-relationship odds: STRONG. UK-HQ + US-expansion-planned is the 'we need a US PR partner' inflection point — Series A companies expanding into US almost always hire fractional or contract US-based comms because their UK PR shops can't penetrate US trade press effectively. Pitch threading: (i) 'AI is breaking expert networks — here's how' narrative for FS-research trade press (Institutional Investor, Pensions & Investments, Hedgeweek, AlphaWeek), (ii) DeepMind-founder origin story for AI-research press (The Information, Wired, MIT Tech Review), (iii) US-expansion launch sequence (cold-launch press in 6–8 weeks, NY office announcement, customer logo-reveal cadence), (iv) thought-leadership on 'voice-first AI onboarding' as a UX category for B2B SaaS press (TheStack, SaaStr, Digiday). Comms-team check: 5/6 TechCrunch quotes founders only; no comms head named anywhere in TFN, Tech.eu, FinSMEs coverage. Outreach lead-touch: James Lo (more press-comfortable per McKinsey/SoftBank backgrounds); Mankowitz is the technical-narrative authority. Time-sensitive: peak outreach 5/6–5/16. Combined score 84 — DEFINITELY pursue. Differentiator vs. typical Series A pitch: lead with FS-buyer-audience expertise, not generic AI startup PR.
leads.lastActivity: $22.75M Series A announced May 6, 2026 (TechCrunch / TheNextWeb / FinSMEs / TechFundingNews / Tech.eu / NewsBytes)

leads.name: Astrocade
leads.industry: AI-Powered Social/UGC Gaming Platform — B2C with AI-creation tools (natural-language game-creation platform; 20M users, 75K+ games, 80 countries — eight months post-launch)
leads.fundingStage: $56M combined funding announced May 5, 2026: Series B led by Sequoia Capital + Series A led by Sea Ltd. Other backers: Google AI Futures Fund, NVIDIA, LG Technology Ventures, Dentsu Ventures, Conviction Embed, Chaac Ventures, Rogue VC.

contacts.name: Fei-Fei Li
contacts.title: Chief Strategy Officer (renowned AI scholar; high-profile addition; press-magnet by herself)

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-08 (Fortune / Yahoo Finance / FinSMEs / Pulse 2.0 / The SaaS News / Astrocade blog)
leads.notes: B-tier (combined 77). B2C-leaning — and that's the discount. Astrocade's primary audience is gamers/creators, not the FS/B2B-buyer profile Mark's playbook is built around, so industry/buyer scores are softer (7/6). BUT three things keep it on the report rather than pruning it out: (a) Fei-Fei Li as CSO is genuinely a press-magnet — she alone generates 6+ months of authored thought-leadership opportunities at WSJ/NYT/MIT Tech Review/Wired, (b) Sequoia + Sea + Google AI + NVIDIA is a heavyweight cap table that signals a big budget for the launch-cycle, (c) the 20M-user / 75K-games / 80-countries traction-numbers are unambiguous proof for any consumer-tech press pitch. Where this becomes interesting for Mark: if the agency wants to expand the consumer-tech book (gaming, creator-economy, AI-creative-tools) this is the entry point for that book — it's a one-deal portfolio expansion. Comms-relationship odds: MODERATE-STRONG. Founders are technical (CTO + CEO Sadeghian brothers), Fei-Fei Li is press-active in academia but not as company spokesperson historically — there's a clear gap for a comms-strategy partner to operationalize her into the company's PR engine. Pitch threading (if pursued): (i) Fei-Fei Li thought-leadership operations (placed essays, podcast bookings, conference panels for AI ethics + creative AI tracks), (ii) creator-economy narrative for digiday/AdAge/MarTech beat, (iii) 'AI gaming as the new consumer interface' for general consumer-tech press. RECOMMEND only if Mark wants to deliberately expand into consumer/gaming. Combined score 77 — B-tier. Time-sensitive but lower-priority than Fazeshift and Ethos.
leads.lastActivity: $56M combined Series A+B announced May 5, 2026 (Fortune / Yahoo Finance / FinSMEs / Pulse 2.0 / The SaaS News / Astrocade blog)

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-08 (ACC Jobline — Association of Corporate Counsel)
leads.notes: B-tier (combined 71). This is a clean, Mark-direct-pitch fractional-GC opportunity for the legal-freelance pipeline. Why it qualifies despite NOT being a fund/private-funds role (which would normally pull the higher +20 fit-bucket): (a) it's an UNAMBIGUOUSLY fractional role — the listing explicitly says 'monthly retainer or fractional hourly basis, 1–2 days per week' (Bucket B + Bucket C of the freelance role taxonomy hit cleanly), (b) workstream-fit is solid — commercial contracts review/redline/negotiation, supply-chain, employment, real-estate, outside-counsel coordination matches Mark's stated practice areas, (c) the company is in growth mode (200 employees, doubling workforce, $300M revenue) which means legal-infrastructure spend is rising and the fractional engagement could expand. Where it scores lower: NOT a fund/private-funds role (the ICP's highest-value bucket), industrial-manufacturing buyer-type is outside Mark's typical fund/PE/portco target audience, and 'Hayden Industrial LLC' isn't a name that surfaces in the major PE/VC/fintech ecosystems Mark networks in. Net: a credible apply-now opportunity as a sole-engagement client at $5–8k/mo retainer tier (~$60–96k annual range based on stated 1–2 days/week + monthly-retainer comp model). Engagement model: fractional (Bucket B explicit). Buyer type: operating-company (industrial manufacturer). Recommended action: Mark applies directly via the ACC Jobline link in the next 48 hours; positioning emphasis on (i) commercial-contracts depth, (ii) growth-stage scaling experience, (iii) availability for sustained 1–2 days/week engagement. Combined 71 — B-tier. ALSO worth doing: scrape ACC Jobline's full active-listings list weekly going forward — the ACC board surfaces fractional/interim postings that don't appear on Indeed/CareerBuilder.
leads.lastActivity: Active listing on ACC Jobline (5/8 verified live); listing date not publicly stated but role is currently open per Association of Corporate Counsel job board

trendsSpotted: Three consecutive weeks of 'YC alum / Series A or B / no in-house comms named' pattern: Reserv (5/4, 87) → Corgi (5/7, 89) → Fazeshift (5/8, 91). All three are post-YC, fintech/insurtech, founder-fronted, and the funding-announcement coverage names ONLY the founders. This is the agency's strongest inbound-signal pattern — recommend formalizing a 'YC-fintech-fresh-Series-A' watchlist.

F-Prime Capital is showing up across multiple May leads (Fazeshift). F-Prime portfolio has 30+ active fintech/AI-fintech companies and the firm's portcos historically retain external PR within 60 days of Series A. Worth targeting F-Prime's IR/portfolio-services contact for partnership conversations.

Job-board freelance volume softer this week — only 1 new strong fractional-GC posting (Hayden Industrial) and 0 new strong PR-freelance postings scoring 60+. ACC Jobline appears to be a higher-quality source than Indeed for fractional-counsel roles; ICP refresh should add ACC Jobline as a primary source.

Late-stage rounds dominated 5/5–5/7 news cycle (Corgi $160M, Astranis $450M, RadixArk $100M seed-but-priced-as-B, JuliaHub $65M). Late-stage post-funding press is captured by incumbent agencies; Mark's fit-target remains the early Series A / first-major-funding moment, which Fazeshift exemplifies cleanly today.
